topStories.description: Leo Tolstoy's inspiring words serves a reminder that the two most powerful warriors are patience and time. While patience keeps us grounded in reality, time helps us in moving forward. The quote is extremely relevant in today's time, when everyone wants results instantly.

topStories.title: Iran says US and Israel attacked Natanz nuclear facility
topStories.description: No leakage of radioactive materials reported in the area in central Iran, Tehran's atomic energy organisation says.

topStories.title: West Asia: US, Israeli strikes target Iran's Natanz nuclear facility, IAEA says no increase in off-site radiation levels
topStories.description: The US and Israel launched an attack on Saturday (local time), targeting Iran's Natanz uranium-enrichment facility. However, no radioactive leaks occurred, Reuters reported, citing Tasnim news agency.

topStories.title: US jury finds Elon Musk misled investors during Twitter purchase
topStories.description: Jury finds that two tweets posted in May 2022 by Musk contained false statements.

dailyNews.title: US & its allies' ‘attempts to silence voice’: Iranian-linked hacking group as its website back after FBI seized domains
dailyNews.description: Following a cyberattack on Stryker, the Iranian-linked Handala Hack Team's website went back online after the FBI seized its domains. The group claimed the seizures were attempts to suppress their voice, while Stryker is working to restore its systems and confirmed product safety.

dailyNews.title: Illuru Lakshmaiah elected president of A.P. Arya Vysya Mahasabha
Refrain from habitual criticism, Congress tells BRS
Congress fields son of BJP leader in southern Assam
dailyNews.description: Rahul Roy, a former MLA, was almost inactive in the grand old party after contesting the 2021 election as an Independent

dailyNews.title: Duggar couple arrested: What are the new charges against Joseph Duggar and Kendra Duggar?
dailyNews.description: Former reality star Joseph Duggar will be transferred to Florida to face child molestation charges after waiving his extradition hearing. He faces serious allegations, including false imprisonment, mirroring past legal troubles of his brother, Josh Duggar, who was convicted of child sexual abuse.

dailyNews.title: Iranian man, Romanian woman arrested trying to enter UK's Falsane nuclear base amid Israel-US' conflict with Iran
dailyNews.description: The man and the woman are due to appear at Dumbarton Sheriff Court on Monday. It also mentioned that enquiries are currently underway

dailyNews.title: Call From Amit Shah Saved NR Congress-BJP Alliance In Puducherry: Sources
dailyNews.description: Despite the truce, the BJP has not conceded either of the NR Congress' two principal demands - statehood and the exclusion of a party from the alliance.

He condemned attacks on infrastructure, warning they disrupt global supply chains and stressed the need to secure shipping routes.

dailyNews.title: War spirals as information control tightens
dailyNews.description: From Washington to Tehran to Tel Aviv, the fight to control the narrative seems as critical as the war itself.

dailyNews.title: 'Shipping lanes must remain open, secure': PM Modi speaks with Iran President, second since West Asia war
dailyNews.description: PM Modi said, “Spoke with President Dr. Masoud Pezeshkian and conveyed Eid and Nowruz greetings.”

dailyNews.title: 'BTS is everything for us': K-pop fans gather in Seoul for comeback show
dailyNews.description: Some 260,000 fans are expected to watch BTS perform together on Saturday for the first time since 2022.

dailyNews.title: US probes Colombian president's alleged drug trafficking ties, designates Gustavo Petro as 'priority target'
dailyNews.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro is under DEA investigation for alleged ties to drug traffickers, including the Sinaloa cartel. The DEA has labeled him a priority target, though Petro denies any wrongdoing.

dailyNews.title: Iran war: What’s happening on day 22 of US-Israel attacks?
dailyNews.description: As conflict enters week 4, here’s the latest on the US-Israel attack on Iran and across the Middle East.

dailyNews.title: Russian drone attack kills two in Ukraine ahead of talks in US, officials say
dailyNews.description: Two children, aged 11 and 15 were also injured in the attack which took place in Zaporizhzhia.

dailyNews.title: Fake Google security page can turn your browser into a spying tool
dailyNews.description: Security researchers say a phishing scam impersonates Google to install malware that steals 2FA codes, tracks location and spies on clipboard data.

dailyNews.title: DAVID MARCUS: Democrat logic: Chicago raises hotel tax ... to attract tourists
dailyNews.description: Chicago's new hotel tax hike raises room rates to 19% to fund Choose Chicago, but critics argue the move will hurt working-class tourists, not help them.

dailyNews.title: Creepy robot mom that gives birth is training future midwives
dailyNews.description: Meet Mama Anne, a lifelike childbirth simulator that blinks, breathes and talks, helping midwifery students practice real labor scenarios safely.

dailyNews.title: Families of Iran's elite live lavishly abroad while ordinary citizens suffer at home
dailyNews.description: Critics say children of Iran's ruling elite live freely in the West while their families enforce strict rules at home, with calls to sanction them.

dailyNews.title: Iran funding emerges as key test for Johnson's razor-thin House majority
dailyNews.description: GOP fiscal hawks warn that a potential $200 billion funding request for the Iran campaign must be offset by spending cuts as the national debt surpasses a staggering $39 trillion.

topStories.description: The war has complicated the Republican message on affordability. Democrats see an opportunity to drive their economic message while tying President Trump to an unpopular overseas conflict.

topStories.title: Trump’s Ballroom Architect Is Skilled at Shrugging Off Outrage
topStories.description: Shalom Baranes, who arrived in the U.S. as a Libyan refugee and went on to renovate the Pentagon, is baffling fellow architects for accepting the job. But he said some of his previous projects have drawn outrage too.

topStories.description: As they sought to respond to charges of antisemitism and harassment on campus, administrators and trustees made promises. Many have been kept, but not all.

topStories.description: Sandro Castro has drawn scrutiny for his Instagram posts in which he flaunts a life of luxury while using satire to point out the very deterioration his family’s leadership helped create.

topStories.title: Trump Is Hiding the Truth About the War in Iran
topStories.description: Whatever short-term gain the president thinks he is getting by lying about the war in Iran is far exceeded by the cost, for him, the country and the world.

topStories.description: Three Opinion writers on Iran, the SAVE Act and the president’s folly.

dailyNews.title: Trump is strategizing means to seize Iran's nuclear stockpiles, sources say
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration has been strategizing methods and options to secure or extract Iran's nuclear materials, according to multiple sources, as the military campaign against Tehran enters a more uncertain phase.

dailyNews.title: Trump weighs deploying ground troops
dailyNews.description: President Trump is weighing whether to send possibly thousands of U.S. troops into Iran as he looks for a way to achieve some of his key goals and end the war, according to the two current U.S. officials, two former U.S. officials and another person familiar with the discussions. NBC News’ Courtney Kube reports.

dailyNews.title: Intelligence chiefs' testimony is out of sync with Trump's talking points
dailyNews.description: For the first time since the U.S. launched a war on Iran, this week top intelligence officials spoke publicly about the conflict.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.

dailyNews.title: Beijing Signals Readiness to Talk to Trump's Team, Even Old Foes
dailyNews.description: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.

dailyNews.title: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
dailyNews.description: The drug is increasingly manufactured in and trafficked from the U.S.’s northern neighbor, though the numbers remain small compared with Mexico.

dailyNews.title: Iran live updates: Trump says he is considering 'winding down' Iran military efforts
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump announced "major combat operations" against Iran on Feb. 28, with massive joint U.S.-Israel strikes.

dailyNews.title: Analysis: Trump's unshackled presidency puts him at the center of the economy
dailyNews.description: The president's unprecedented use of executive power has made him a driving force behind oil prices, the Federal Reserve's interest rates and more.

dailyNews.title: Opinion: As Trump eyes Cuba, my trips there a decade ago remind me how different things were
dailyNews.description: Trump has threatened a 'friendly takeover' of Cuba after cutting it off from Venezuela's oil. CNBC's Justin Solomon reflects how different things used to be.

dailyNews.title: Underground Railroad museum says Trump administration cut funds over race
dailyNews.description: The founders of an Underground Railroad history site in Albany, N.Y., claim the Trump administration’s funding cuts were aimed at marginalizing Black history.

dailyNews.title: Firm with ties to Trump officials signs deal to lobby for Libyan warlord
dailyNews.description: Ballard Partners will represent Khalifa Hifter and his armed forces, signaling a renegade commander’s desire to gain influence in Washington.
